objections.description: Artifact is truncated mid-cell in the contrast matrix: `| --text-link #8B7`. The contrast compliance verification for link colors, success/error/warning colors, and light theme matrix are all missing. Cannot verify WCAG compliance without complete data.
objections.suggested_fix: Complete the contrast compliance matrix for both themes, all semantic colors, and all background combinations.

objections.description: Artifact ends mid-section. Convergence criteria require approval of: motion behavior, accessibility compliance, component structure. Sections 3.2-3.8 (typography, spacing, elevation, radius, motion, breakpoints, FOUC prevention), Section 4 (core layout shell), Section 5 (key screens), Section 6 (skeleton/motion system) are all missing. This is Round 5 — the spec should be complete.
objections.suggested_fix: Provide complete spec including: typography tokens, spacing scale, elevation system, border-radius tokens, motion/animation system with explicit timing functions (no ease/linear per requirements), responsive breakpoint implementations, and all 3 required screens (Dashboard, Document/Timeline, ActionSheet).

objections.description: Bundle size checker only validates JS files. CSS is not measured but contributes to total transfer size. A 160KB JS budget is meaningless if CSS adds another 50KB+. Also, `gzipSync({ level: 9 })` uses maximum compression which CDN/browsers typically don't use (usually level 6), making budgets optimistically small.
objections.suggested_fix: Add CSS file checking to the bundle script. Use level 6 compression to match real-world CDN behavior. Add CSS budget (suggest ≤20KB gzipped for initial load).

objections.description: Code-splitting lazy imports show incorrect relative paths: `React.lazy(() => import('./features/search/SearchScreen'))` from `app/routes.js` would resolve to `app/features/search/SearchScreen` but the actual path is `src/features/search/SearchScreen`. Need correct relative path from app/routes.js.
objections.suggested_fix: Lazy imports should use `React.lazy(() => import('../features/search/SearchScreen'))` from routes.js location.

objections.description: Manual chunking only separates vendor. For a 3-screen app with lazy loading, feature chunks should be defined to prevent a single lazy chunk exceeding 40KB. Search, Dashboard, and Document screens should be separate chunks.
objections.suggested_fix: Add explicit chunk strategy: `manualChunks: { vendor: ['react', 'react-dom'], search: () => import('./features/search/DashboardScreen'), dashboard: () => import('./features/dashboard/DashboardScreen'), document: () => import('./features/document/DocumentScreen') }` or rely on dynamic imports with named output.

objections.description: Middleware sets immutable cache on all `/assets/*` paths but doesn't verify files actually have content hashes. If Vite config changes or build fails partway, unhashed files could be cached incorrectly.
objections.suggested_fix: Add validation in check-bundle-size.js that all files in assets/ contain content hashes (pattern: `[name]-[hash][ext]`). Fail build if any unhashed files exist.

suggestions.description: Consider pre-bundling react and react-dom into a single vendor chunk during build rather than treating them separately. Vite does this automatically but the budget table shows them as separate line items.
The navigation direction detection logic (`useNavigationDirection`) is mentioned but not implemented. This needs actual code to track history position changes.
The FOUC prevention inline script is referenced but not shown. This is critical for dark-mode-first approach. Include the actual script block.
